questions.question: Roughly how many tokens does a 30-minute clip cost at 1 FPS with 3x3 pooling (81 tokens/frame)?
options: About 5,000 tokens
1800 frames × 81 = 145,800 tokens — doable by 2025 open VLMs but tight; doubling to 2 FPS hits 291,600, and a 2-hour movie at 1 FPS reaches 583k tokens
Exactly 1 million tokens
Under 100 tokens after compression
questions.explanation: Token arithmetic is the gatekeeper of long-video work: FPS × duration × tokens-per-frame decides which scaling path you're forced onto before any model choice matters.

questions.question: What is the "brute context" path, and what recall did Gemini 1.5 document?
options: Caching answers to common questions

Scale context to millions of tokens and process everything in one pass — Gemini 1.5 documented 99.7% needle-in-a-haystack recall up to ~9.5M tokens, via unpublished custom attention with memory hierarchy plus MoE routing
Re-watching the video multiple times
Trimming every video to 60 seconds
questions.explanation: It's the highest-recall path and the least reproducible: the engineering isn't published in full detail and isn't open-source — hence the other three open paths.

questions.question: How does ring attention make million-token training feasible?
options: It skips attention for most tokens

The sequence is chunked across devices in a ring; each device passes its chunk along, computing partial attention and aggregating — the quadratic cost is amortized across devices, so training compute scales linearly with context
It quantizes attention weights to 1 bit
It only attends to the first and last frames
questions.explanation: LWM trained a 1M-token context model this way; LongVILA adapted it to VLMs, handling 1400-frame videos (268k context) with 8-way ring parallelism.

questions.question: What does token compression (Video-XL's summary tokens) trade away?
options: Nothing — it's strictly better
Recall at specific timestamps: each N-frame clip collapses to a single summary token, so the model knows generally what happened but can miss exact frames
The ability to process color video
Compatibility with transformer architectures
questions.explanation: Compression is the cheap path — the LLM sees one token per clip instead of thousands. LongVA's complementary trick: extend context on TEXT (200k→2M) and transfer it to video via shared representations.

questions.question: How does VideoAgent differ from all three context-scaling paths?
options: It uses a bigger context than Gemini
It never feeds the full video to the LLM — the video becomes a database, and the LLM agentically queries a retrieval tool for relevant clips ("show me segments with a cat") before answering
It transcribes the video to text only
It fine-tunes a new model per video
questions.explanation: Agentic retrieval swaps raw context for tool calls: much cheaper per question and unbounded in video length, at the cost of retrieval misses and multi-step latency.
